features.summary: Broadcom reported record Q4 2021 revenue of $7.4 billion, up 15% year-over-year, driven by 17% growth in semiconductor solutions to $5.6 billion and 8% growth in infrastructure software to $1.8 billion. Semiconductor gross margins expanded 170 basis points to 70% despite higher wireless mix. The company guided Q1 2022 revenue to $7.6 billion with continued double-digit semiconductor growth of 17% year-over-year. Free cash flow was strong at $3.5 billion representing 47% of revenue.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued momentum across all semiconductor segments in fiscal 2022, with networking firing on all cylinders and accelerating to 30% year-over-year growth in Q1. Enterprise demand recovery is driving 30% growth in server storage connectivity, while broadband maintains double-digit growth rates. The company is booked through 2022 and into 2023 with 50+ week lead times remaining stable. Infrastructure software is expected to sustain mid-single digit growth with strong renewal rates of 116% overall and 127% for strategic accounts.
features.risks: Extended lead times of 50+ weeks create potential for demand volatility when conditions normalize, though management believes disciplined allocation prevents excess inventory buildup. Supply constraints could limit ability to meet strong demand across all segments. Wireless business faces typical consumer seasonality challenges beyond Q1, with April quarter demand difficult to forecast. The sustainability of elevated hypercloud spending levels and enterprise recovery pace presents execution risks.
features.key_drivers: Strong enterprise spending recovery is driving 30%+ growth in networking, server storage, and industrial segments after weak first half 2021. Hypercloud customers are expanding beyond traditional switching/routing into AI compute offload applications, growing the ASIC business toward $2 billion. 5G infrastructure deployment and Wi-Fi 6/6E adoption continue driving broadband growth. The company announced a $10 billion share repurchase program and increased quarterly dividend 14% to $4.10, reflecting confidence in cash generation capabilities.
